results.title: Wanted: dead or alive
results.creator_credit: Harry Harris Jr, Thomas Carr, Ed Adamson, John Robinson and RBC Entertainment
results.brief_description: This tape contains two episodes of the cult TV series, starring Steve McQueen as Josh Randall, the bounty hunter with a heart. In Episode 5 : “8 Cent reward”, on Christmas Eve a little boy seeks out Josh in a bar to search for a very special man, Santa Claus. Since he doesn’t want to disappoint the boy he tries to at least help him in some way - but even the best laid plans can fail. In Episode 6 : “The Choice”, Jane Koster asks Josh for help - her husband Frank is also a bounty hunter, but he isn’t as young or as fast as he used to be. He is trying to settle a score with a dangerous criminal and his wife fears he may be killed, sooner rather than later.

results.title: Wandering Spirit Survival School
results.creator_credit: Christopher Wilson, Les Holdway, Marvin Midwicki, Lisa Turcot and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: This school, organized by concerned parents, broke with tradition by introducing subjects that are of particular relevance to its pupils. Indian legends, traditions, languages and crafts balance the program of academic subjects required by the Ontario Ministry of Education. The experience of the children at Wandering Spirit is contrasted with the very different life experienced by their parents, educated in the old residential schools.

results.title: Waltzing Matilda (Captioned)
results.brief_description: In this humorous claymation dramatisation of Banjo Patterson’s famous poem, all of the characters, with the exception of the ill-fated sheep thief, are transformed into animals. Shot on a set designed to replicate Russell Drysdale’s Outback paintings, the film brings to life and explains the terminology of the lyrics.

results.title: Waltzing Matilda
results.creator_credit: Gunnar Isakson, Geoffrey Mill, Australian Broadcasting Commission and Mill Productions
results.brief_description: The traditional ballad by A.B. Paterson sung to a modern tune and guitar accompaniament, and acted in bush surroundings. Sung by Beth Schurr; Swagman played by Geoffrey Mill. Suitable for primary school level.

results.title: Walter the lazy mouse
results.creator_credit: Stanislaw Dulz, Doubleday Productions and Viking Films
results.brief_description: Based on the book of the same title by Marjorie Flack. An animated story about a lazy mouse who learns to provide for himself as he searches for his family which has moved without telling him. Illustrated by Cyndy Szekeres. Narrated by Bob Dishy.

results.title: Walter Lindrum Billiards Phenomenon
results.brief_description: The Great Depression seemed to be the catalyst for the most remarkable chapter in Australian sporting history. During the very depths of the Depression an elite group of our greatest champions reach the pinnacle of their success. Phar Lap and Sir Donald Bradman are the two best known heroes from this period. Sir Henry Opperman was a third member. The fourth, and least known of the fabulous four was Billiards King, Walter Lindrum. The program tells of his humble beginnings and how he overcame hardship and tragedy to completely dominate his chosen sport.

results.title: Walter Kerr, on theater
results.creator_credit: Joseph Anthony, Robert Saudek and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: Walter Kerr, New York Times art critic, comments on dramatic scenes from five plays and compares them with film segments, pointing out the unique character of live performance, and showing the range of forms and ideas in live theatre. Play excerpts include: “Prometheus” by Aeschylus, “Richard III” by William Shakespeare, “the Importance of being Ernest” by Oscar Wilde, The Serpent” by Jean-Claude van Itallie and “No Place to be Somebody by Charles Gordone. Narrated by Walter Kerr.

results.title: Walter and Milly went to read (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: Karl McPhee, Tom Manefield and Film Australia
results.brief_description: Study of the reading abilities of children. Walter and Milly are ordinary children encountering the developmental tasks of their respective ages, with the usual successes and failures. Their parents and teachers try to create the best learning environment for the children. Photographer, John Hoskins; editor, Mark Waters; sound, Max Henser, Julian Ellingworth; music, Michael Carlos.
